sets.q: A 100 W light bulb is left on for 5 hours. How many kilowatt-hours of electrical energy does it consume?
sets.given:
Formula: E (kWh) = Power (kW) × Time (h)
Convert watts to kilowatts: 100 W = 0.1 kW

sets.q: A 200 W fan runs for 3 hours. How many kilowatt-hours does it consume?
A 500 W washing machine runs for 2 hours. How many kWh does it consume?
A 1 500 W air conditioner runs for 4 hours. How many kWh does it use?
A 250 W television is watched for 6 hours. How many kWh does it consume?
A household uses 4 kWh of electricity. If the rate is ₱12 per kWh, how much does the electricity cost?
A family uses 25 kWh of electricity. The rate is ₱10 per kWh. What is the total cost?
An appliance consumes 8 kWh. The electricity rate is ₱15 per kWh. What is the cost?
A store uses 150 kWh per month. The rate is ₱11 per kWh. What is the monthly electricity bill?
A school consumes 500 kWh in a month. At ₱9 per kWh, what is the total bill?
A 2 000 W electric iron is used for 30 minutes. How many kWh does it consume?
sets.given:
E = P(kW) × t(h)
2 000 W = 2 kW; 30 min = 0.5 h

sets.q: A 1 000 W microwave is used for 15 minutes. How many kWh does it consume?
A 3 000 W electric stove is used for 45 minutes daily. How many kWh per day?
A 600 W blender runs for 10 minutes. How many kWh does it use?
A 800 W water heater runs for 20 minutes. How many kWh does it consume?
What does kWh stand for?
sets.given: This is the standard unit used by electric utilities to measure energy consumption.

sets.q: 1 kWh is equivalent to how many joules?
Why do electricity companies use kWh instead of joules for billing?
sets.given: Consider the scale of energy used in a typical household.
opts: kWh is a larger and more practical unit for household energy use
kWh is smaller and easier to count
Joules are only for mechanical energy

sets.q: A 1 kW appliance running for 1 hour consumes:
On an electricity bill, energy consumption is typically measured in:
sets.given: Think about the unit used to calculate electric bills.

sets.q: A 60 W bulb is used 8 hours per day for 30 days. What is the total energy consumed in kWh?
sets.given:
E = P(kW) × t(h)
60 W = 0.06 kW; total hours = 8 × 30 = 240 h

sets.q: A 100 W fan is used 6 hours per day for 30 days. How many kWh does it consume?
A 400 W air cooler runs 5 hours per day for 30 days. Total energy consumed?
A 1 200 W refrigerator runs 24 hours per day. How many kWh does it use in 30 days?
A 50 W router runs all day (24 hours) for 30 days. Total energy in kWh?
A 60 W bulb is used 8 hours per day for 30 days. At ₱12/kWh, what is the monthly cost?
sets.given:
Step 1: E = 0.06 kW × 240 h = 14.4 kWh
Step 2: Cost = 14.4 × ₱12

sets.q: A 200 W lamp is on 5 hours daily for 30 days. At ₱10/kWh, what is the cost?
A 1 500 W AC runs 4 hours daily for 30 days. At ₱11/kWh, what is the monthly cost?
A 400 W TV is on 6 hours daily for 30 days. At ₱9/kWh, what is the cost?
A 50 W charger runs 3 hours per day for 30 days. At ₱12/kWh, what is the cost?
Which appliance is the most expensive to run for 1 hour? Rate: ₱10/kWh
Which of the following uses the least energy in 1 hour?
sets.given: Lower power rating = less energy per hour.

sets.q: A 10 W LED and a 60 W incandescent bulb both run for 1 hour daily for 30 days at ₱12/kWh. What is the difference in monthly cost?
A family replaces a 100 W bulb with a 10 W LED, using it 5 hours/day for 30 days at ₱12/kWh. How much do they save?
sets.given: Savings = ΔP × t × rate = (0.1−0.01) kW × 150 h × 12

sets.q: Which habit saves the most electricity?
sets.given: Consider which action reduces the most power consumption.
opts: Replacing a 2 000 W AC with a 1 200 W inverter AC (800 W saved)
Turning off a 10 W phone charger (10 W saved)
Using a 60 W instead of 100 W bulb (40 W saved)
Unplugging a 5 W standby device (5 W saved)

sets.q: A household's electricity bill shows 180 kWh used. At ₱12/kWh, what is the bill amount?
An electricity bill shows 350 kWh consumed at ₱10.50/kWh. What is the total cost?
A dormitory uses 420 kWh per month at ₱11/kWh. What is the monthly bill?
A sari-sari store uses 95 kWh in a month at ₱13/kWh. What is the bill?
A school building uses 2 500 kWh per month. At ₱9.50/kWh, what is the electricity bill?
A family paid ₱1 080 for electricity at ₱12/kWh. How many kWh did they consume?
An electric bill of ₱2 500 was charged at ₱10/kWh. How many kWh were used?
A bill of ₱1 650 was issued at ₱11/kWh. What was the energy consumed?
A store paid ₱4 500 for electricity at ₱9/kWh. How much energy did they use?
A homeowner received a ₱3 000 bill at ₱12.50/kWh. How many kWh were consumed?
A barangay hall has 10 × 40 W bulbs, 2 × 200 W fans, and 1 × 1 000 W projector all running for 4 hours. What is the total energy consumed?
A classroom has 8 × 20 W LED bulbs and 2 × 60 W fans running for 6 hours. What is the total energy?
A small shop runs 5 × 100 W bulbs and 1 × 500 W air cooler for 8 hours. Total energy consumed?
A house uses 3 × 60 W bulbs, 1 × 150 W TV, and 1 × 1 500 W AC for 5 hours. What is the energy consumed?
An office has 20 × 10 W LED bulbs and 10 × 100 W desktop computers running for 9 hours. Total energy?
